title: Match Docker pull/push by defaulting to progress write to stdout
body:
Fixes: https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/21181

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Podman pull and push progress now goes to stdout.
```

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *rhatdan* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/OWNERS)~~ [rhatdan] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

I am not sure how desirable this really is? It would break all scripts doing something like this: https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/19454#discussioncomment-6604854
And the conclusion there was to not change it. Of course with 5.0 we have the option to break it but I personally don't think we should.

I guess the question is whether we want better support in "Azure DevOps pipeline" via breaking existing tools.

Azure might do a better job of checking failures, if we ask. We should probably discuss this in a meeting.

Whatever decision we make, I urge to record it and maybe document it in the man pages.

What I don't want to is to choose between breaking either (class) of users.  Maybe add a new flag instead that allows for switching the behavior?  Maybe Azure can just run with via `--quiet`?  Certainly, Azure pipelines are important and huge but so are existing users and workloads.

I think @Luap99 is right; we can’t change the stdout format any more.

The argument that Docker does use stdout has _some_ value, but we already differ from Docker in writing the image ID; and that is only really valuable if it is the only output to stdout. (Hypothetically, we could have decided to write it to a separate file, like `podman push --digestfile` does, but we are now stuck with the current output format.)

---

I’m inclined to say the Azure error handling detection , alluded to in #21181 , is just wrong, and we shouldn’t bend over backwards for it. The Unix ~meme of using pipelines to connect programs necessarily implies that progress messages tend to go to stderr, as a _general_ principle. Meanwhile, we have exit codes to _reliably_ indicate outcome of a program.

(Compare https://github.com/containers/podman/discussions/19454#discussioncomment-6608431 .)

Of course any particular software can decide on some other semantics of stdin/out/err, exit codes, or anything else, but I think it’s valuable for all software on a platform to behave consistently, if possible; and I think it’s _completely unsustainable_ to change behavior just because _one_ consumer in the 8-billion-people-universe makes an assumption. If a similarly big company comes around next month, asking for the behavior changed back, are we… going to run an auction? 😈 

(I have no opinion on adding an option to redirect the output.)
I tend to agree with @mtrmac and @Luap99 - this has been part of our CLI since 1.0 and seems more correct than what Docker is doing, so I don't see a strong motivation to change.
